title: BlueDot Narrated
summary: Narrated audio of BlueDot's curated AI safety articles — a good general listen across the safety landscape.

title: AI could defeat all of us combined
summary: Short audio essay laying out the case that an unaligned advanced AI could pose a threat at civilizational scale.

title: Future of Life Institute
summary: Long-running org working on existential and catastrophic risks, including AI. A good place to follow advocacy and research at the policy/risk intersection.

title: Gradual disempowerment summary
summary: Accessible summary of the 'gradual disempowerment' argument — that humanity could lose meaningful control even without a sudden takeover.

title: A Framework for Evaluating Emerging Cyberattack Capabilities of AI
summary: Paper proposing a framework for evaluating how AI systems advance offensive cyber capabilities — useful for thinking about evaluations and defence.

title: What hackers talk about when they talk about AI
summary: Empirical paper studying early-stage diffusion of AI-enabled cybercrime techniques in hacker communities.

title: AI in Cybersecurity: A Fireside Chat with Bruce Schneier
summary: Conversational blogpost with Schneier on how AI is changing offence and defence in cybersecurity.

title: The future of phishing
summary: Interactive demo from CivAI showing how AI changes the phishing landscape — try it for 15 minutes to feel the threat directly.

title: BlueDot — Biosecurity course
summary: Structured part-time BlueDot course on biosecurity in the age of AI.

title: Future of Life — autonomous weapons site
summary: Hub site from FLI on autonomous weapons — explainers, advocacy materials, and policy resources.

title: Existential Risk from Power-Seeking AI (audio essay)
summary: Carlsmith's careful, accessible audio essay laying out the case that power-seeking AI poses an existential risk. Good single entry-point to the argument.

title: An Overview of Catastrophic AI Risks
summary: Light-read survey of the main categories of catastrophic AI risk — useful as a first map of the territory.

title: Opportunities to Strengthen U.S. Biosecurity from AI-Enabled Bioterrorism
summary: Policy analysis on what governments can do to harden biosecurity against AI-amplified bioterrorism risks.

title: Risks from Biological Research
summary: Two leading biosecurity researchers discuss how AI changes the landscape of biorisk, and what to do about it.

title: Mitigating catastrophic biorisks
summary: Talk on what concrete mitigations look like for AI-amplified biorisks.

title: A Biosecurity and Biorisk Reading List
summary: Curated reading list spanning biosecurity policy, dual-use research, and AI's role.

title: Lethal Autonomous Weapon Systems research directory (up to 2023)
summary: Directory of academic research on lethal autonomous weapons systems. A starting point for serious reading on the topic.

title: Stop Killer Robots
summary: International coalition advocating against the development and use of lethal autonomous weapons. Good place to find current campaigns and news.

title: Slaughterbots — if human: kill()
summary: Five-minute dramatised short film imagining a future of weaponised drone swarms — designed to make the abstract risk visceral.

title: Career review: biorisk research
summary: What biorisk research careers look like, why they matter as AI lowers the barrier to engineering biothreats, and how to enter the field.

title: Career review: information security
summary: Why infosec is increasingly central to AI safety — protecting model weights, lab security, and critical infrastructure — and how to build a career there.

title: Career review: macrostrategy research
summary: What macrostrategy research is, who funds it, and how it shapes longer-horizon thinking about AI and existential risk.
